mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.5 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving immediate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and industrial production confirms a broad-based economic expansion that validates corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon when both series rise concurrently.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and INDPRO both rise month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistically significant difference between periods where GDPC1 and INDPRO rise together versus periods where they diverge (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During an early monetary tightening cycle, rising short-term rates increase borrowing costs for highly leveraged firms, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns remain positive in over 55% of triggered instances despite rising rates, or the signal exhibits no predictive power outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal strengthening consumer confidence and construction sector activity, which leads equity markets to price in higher near-term corporate earnings and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for HOUST monthly changes against 5-day SPX returns remains below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index exceeding 1.5 percent indicates a supply-side contraction that disrupts corporate supply chains and reduces output expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly lower during months where INDPRO drops above the 1.5% threshold compared to baseline periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in nonfarm payrolls and housing starts confirm synchronized labor market strength and capital expenditure, validating sustained earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS and HOUST both rise month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns show no significant outperformance when PAYEMS and HOUST rise together versus when only one series rises (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion with rising inflation, accelerating consumer prices trigger fears of premature monetary tightening, causing equity multiples to contract and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL accelerates above the threshold, or the signal fails to predict returns during identified late-cycle periods.
